data.narrative:
Heven AeroTech is a Series B-stage defense and security technology company currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of seven same-sector, same-stage private companies, supplemented by two public defense-sector comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implies $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across seven defense & security Series B companies yields a $1.2B median valuation, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B. The peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI at $1.5B, $1.5B, and $1.4B respectively; Code Metal and Castelion anchor the lower end at $1.2B and $1.0B.

The two methods triangulate to a range of $1.1B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The divergence between stage-progression ($1.9B) and peer-median ($1.2B) reflects uncertainty regarding Heven's relative competitive positioning within its cohort. Key unknowns include revenue scale relative to peers, defensibility of core IP, and customer concentration in an evolving defense procurement environment. A valuation in the $1.4B–$1.7B range appears most defensible absent additional operating metrics.
